info.title: Webhooks
info.description: OpenAPI specification for Zoho CRM Webhooks (settings/automation) — endpoints to create, list, update, delete webhooks, inspect failures and usage reports. Targets CRM v8 automation workflows and integrations.

get.description: Retrieve all available webhooks configured in your Zoho CRM account.

403.description:
When the user doesn't have permission.
(manageworkflow)

put.description: To update an existing webhook available in your Zoho CRM account.

403.description:
When the user doesn't have permission.
(manageworkflow)

post.description: Webhooks in Zoho CRM allow you to send real-time data from Zoho CRM to external applications or services when specific events occur such as Record creation, update, or deletion.

403.description:
When the user doesn't have permission.
(manageworkflow)

delete.description: To delete one or more webhooks configured in your Zoho CRM account. You can delete up to 10 webhooks in a single API call.

207.description: When one webhook got deleted successfully while other tasks failed

400.description: Only maximum of 10 actions can be deleted

403.description:
When the user doesn't have permission.
(manageworkflow)

get.description: Retrieve the webhooks configured in your Zoho CRM account.

parameters.name: webhookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook to retrieve.

403.description:
When the user doesn't have permission.
(manageworkflow)

put.description: To update an existing webhook available in your Zoho CRM account.

parameters.name: webhookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook to retrieve.

403.description:
When the user doesn't have permission.
(manageworkflow)

delete.description: To delete one webhooks configured in your Zoho CRM account.

parameters.name: webhookId
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook to delete.

403.description:
When the user doesn't have permission.
(manageworkflow)

get.description: To retrieve detailed information about webhook execution failures in your Zoho CRM organization.

400.description: Given from || to date is invalid

403.description:
Permission Denied

When the user doesn't have permission.

(manageworkflow)

get.description: To retrieve the list of modules that are associated with Webhook actions in the current CRM account.
To retrieve usage statistics for Webhook actions executed in Zoho CRM over the last seven days.

403.description:
Permission Denied

When the user doesn't have permission.

(manageworkflow)

WebhookModuleParameters.description: Defines headers with unique names and values for sending additional information via webhook requests, such as API keys or tokens.

name.description: Specify a unique header name. Please note that if you choose Headers, you must add at least one module parameter with a value. If you do not add it, the system will show an error when you try to save the webhook.

value.description: Specify a value of the module header, specified using the merge field format. Example, ${!Leads.Email}.

WebhookModuleParameters.title: Webhook Module Parameters

ErrorResponseCore.description: If given connection name is invalid
ErrorResponseCore.title: Error Response Core

WebhookStatusResponse.description: Represents the status information of a webhook including operational code, message, and status details.
WebhookStatusResponse.title: Webhook Status Response

InvalidActionIDErrorResponse.description: Error due to an invalid action ID provided within a request, requiring validation and resubmission of the correct ID.

enum: the id given seems to be invalid

InvalidActionIDErrorResponse.title: Invalid Action ID Error Response

WebhookHeaders.description: Encapsulates headers for webhook requests including both module-specific and custom parameters, required for sending extra information.

custom_parameters.description: These are static values that never change. They are always sent with the webhook call.

module_parameters.description: Headers let you send extra information along with the webhook request. For example, API keys, authentication tokens, or custom values that the external application needs. (Required)

WebhookHeaders.title: Webhook Headers

InvalidDateTimeFormatErrorResponse.description: Signifies an issue with date-time formatting, advising the correction to a valid and standard format.

enum: The date time format given is invalid

InvalidDateTimeFormatErrorResponse.title: Invalid DateTime Format Error Response

AutomationLockStatusResponse.description: Represents the lock status property of an automation assignment rule, indicating whether the rule is currently locked.
AutomationLockStatusResponse.title: Automation Lock Status Response

InvalidNameErrorResponse.description: Error response indicating the use of invalid characters in a name field, providing feedback on unsupported characters.
InvalidNameErrorResponse.title: Invalid Name Error Response

AmbiguityProcessingErrorResponse.description: Error response indicating a processing ambiguity when module ID and API name do not refer to the same module.
AmbiguityProcessingErrorResponse.title: Ambiguity During Processing Error Response

WebhookCustomParameters.description: Contains static header names and values that are always included in webhook requests for customization.

name.description: Specify a name to the custom header.

value.description: Specify a value to the custom header.

WebhookCustomParameters.title: Webhook Custom Parameters

WebhookDetailsResponse.description: Nested details about a webhook, providing a unique identifier to distinguish it within the system.
WebhookDetailsResponse.title: Webhook Details Response

InvalidTimezoneErrorResponse.description: Indicates an error due to an invalid timezone specification, requiring adjustment to a supported timezone value.
InvalidTimezoneErrorResponse.title: Invalid Timezone Error Response

PaginationInfoResponse.description: Contains pagination metadata such as number of items per page, total count, current page number, and whether more records are available.
PaginationInfoResponse.title: Pagination Information Response

InvalidMethodTypeErrorResponse.description: Error response when an invalid HTTP method is specified, indicating the unavailability of this method for the requested operation.

enum: The method type given is invalid

InvalidMethodTypeErrorResponse.title: Invalid Method Type Error Response

WebhookRequestBody.description: Defines the structure of the request body for a webhook, including data format and content to be sent during POST and PUT operations.

format.description: Specify the format of the body (Required)

raw_data_content.description: Use this when you want to send plain text, JSON, HTML, or XML content directly.

WebhookRequestBody.title: Webhook Request Body

GeneratedSchema2.description: When one webhook got deleted successfully while other tasks failed
GeneratedSchema2.title: Generated Schema

AutomationModuleDetailsResponse.description: Provides details of a module within an automation assignment rule, including API name and module ID.
AutomationModuleDetailsResponse.title: Automation Module Details Response

NameLimitExceededErrorResponse.description: Error response for exceeding the character limit in a name field, indicating the need to shorten the name supplied.

enum: The lenght of name has exceeded the limit

NameLimitExceededErrorResponse.title: Name Character Limit Exceeded Error Response

InvalidDataErrorResponse.description: Error response indicating invalid data was supplied in a request, with details about the error code and message.

enum: The value provided to the param is Invalid

InvalidDataErrorResponse.title: Invalid Data Error Response

DuplicateDataErrorResponse.description: Indicates duplicates in module or custom parameter names, instructing to ensure unique names are used.
DuplicateDataErrorResponse.title: Duplicate Data Error Response

GetWebhooksSuccessResponse.description: Successful response structure for fetching a list of webhooks, including webhook data and pagination information.
GetWebhooksSuccessResponse.title: Get Webhooks Success Response

ErrorResponseCore10.description: If authentication is 'general'  and connection name is given .
ErrorResponseCore10.title: Error Response Core

InvalidDateFormatErrorResponse.description: Error response for issues with date format, directing the user to supply a properly formatted date string.
InvalidDateFormatErrorResponse.title: Invalid Date Format Error Response

UnsupportedMergeFieldErrorResponse.description: Error response when unsupported fields are present, highlighting the usage of unsupported merge fields.
UnsupportedMergeFieldErrorResponse.title: Unsupported Merge Field Error Response

ErrorResponseCore17.description: if value || name is not given in custom parameters

enum: the given parameter is invalid, specify both name and value

ErrorResponseCore17.title: Error Response Core

ErrorResponseCore18.description: If given parameters are not allowed for given http method

enum: Invalid value provided for the given http method

ErrorResponseCore18.title: Error Response Core

ErrorResponseCore13.description: If http method is not given

enum: The method type is not given

ErrorResponseCore13.title: Error Response Core

enum: One of the expected param is missing

AutomationWebhooksResponse.description: Comprehensive details of a webhook related to automation, including creation/modification details, editable status, associated modules, and more.

headers.description: Headers let you send extra information along with the webhook request. For example, API keys, authentication tokens, or custom values that the external application needs.

body.description: Define the data that Zoho CRM sends in the body of the webhook request. This is applicable only for POST and PUT HTTP methods

authentication.description: Specify authentication details for the webhook endpoint. (Required)

AutomationWebhooksResponse.title: Automation Webhooks Response

InvalidDateRangeErrorResponse.description: Error when the provided date range is invalid, advising rectification of 'from' and 'to' dates in the request.

enum: The value provided to the param is Invalid

InvalidDateRangeErrorResponse.title: Invalid Date Range Error Response

CreateWebhooksRequestBody.description: Request body structure for creating new webhooks, including an array of webhook objects with all necessary details.
CreateWebhooksRequestBody.title: Create Webhooks Request Body

InvalidModuleErrorResponse.description: Error response when a module provided in a request is invalid, including error code, status, and descriptive message.

enum: The value provided to the param is Invalid

InvalidModuleErrorResponse.title: Invalid Module Error Response

AutomationModifiedByResponse.description: Details about the person who last modified a specific automation assignment rule, including their name and ID.
AutomationModifiedByResponse.title: Automation Modified By Response

AutomationCreatedByResponse.description: Information about the creator of a specific automation assignment rule, including the creator's name and ID.
AutomationCreatedByResponse.title: Automation Created By Response

ErrorResponseCore9.description: Only maximum of 10 actions can be deleted

enum: Bulk deletion of records limit reached

ErrorResponseCore9.title: Error Response Core

CreateWebhooksSuccessResponse.description: Successful response for a webhook creation request, confirming the status and relevant details of each created webhook.
CreateWebhooksSuccessResponse.title: Create Webhooks Success Response

NoPermissionErrorResponse.description: Error response indicating a user's lack of permissions to perform a specific action on the workflow.
NoPermissionErrorResponse.title: No Permission Error Response

InvalidUrlErrorResponse.description: Error response indicating an invalid URL was entered, advising correction to a valid and well-formed URL.
InvalidUrlErrorResponse.title: Invalid URL Error Response

WebhookAuthenticationDetails.description: Specifies the authentication method and related details for securing a webhook endpoint, ensuring authorized access.
WebhookAuthenticationDetails.title: Webhook Authentication Details

enum: One of the expected parameter is missing
the module id given seems to be invalid

ErrorResponseCore5.description: Locked webhooks can not be edited

enum: Insufficient privileges to edit Read only webhook

ErrorResponseCore5.title: Error Response Core

enum: This webhook is associated with at least one of Approval Process/Workflow Rules/Blueprint

ParamQueryRelatedModule.name: related_module
ParamQueryRelatedModule.description: To filter webhooks triggered by a child or dependent module linked to a parent module. For example, the webhook will be triggered whenever a note (child) is created under a lead (Parent).

ParamQueryTo.name: to
ParamQueryTo.description: The end date in YYYY-MM-DD format, up to which failure records are to be retrieved.

ParamQueryPerPage.name: per_page
ParamQueryPerPage.description: Specify how many webhooks to return per page. The default and the maximum possible value is 200.

ParamQueryWebhookId.name: webhook_id
ParamQueryWebhookId.description: The unique ID of the webhook whose failure details you want to retrieve.

ParamQueryFilters.name: filters
ParamQueryFilters.description: This parameter allows you to filter the webhooks available in your organization by their name.

ParamQueryType.name: type
ParamQueryType.description: Specify the type of resource to filter or group the usage data. This parameter is mandatory when resource is included in the group_by value.

ParamQuerySortBy.name: sort_by
ParamQuerySortBy.description: To sort the webhooks based on the Modified_Time field.

ParamQueryGroupBy.name: group_by
ParamQueryGroupBy.description: Specify the criteria to group the usage data. Please note that date is mandatory, and you can optionally include other values separated by commas.

ParamQueryPage.name: page
ParamQueryPage.description: To get the list of webhooks from the respective pages. The default value is 1. Note that you cannot use this param with the "page_token" param.

ParamQueryFrom.name: from
ParamQueryFrom.description: The start date in YYYY-MM-DD format, from which webhook failure details are to be fetched..

ParamQueryFeatureType.name: feature_type
ParamQueryFeatureType.description: o filter webhooks by their automation feature type. Possible values: workflow , assignmentrules , connectedworkflow

ParamQueryModule.name: module
ParamQueryModule.description: Represents the parent module that the webhook monitors

ParamQuerySortOrder.name: sort_order
ParamQuerySortOrder.description: To sort the available list of webhooks in either ascending or descending order, based on the value of sort_by parameter.

ParamQueryIds.name: ids
ParamQueryIds.description: pecify the webhook IDs that need to be deleted. Use the Get Webhooks API to retrieve all available webhook IDs. A maximum of 10 webhooks can be deleted in a single API call.
